page: Vultr vbm-256c-3072gb-8-mi325x-gpu is a bare metal server configured with AMD EPYC 9575F processors. It features 256 vCPUs across 128 physical cores with a base clock speed of 3.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 3072.0 GB of system memory, providing 12.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server integrates massive hardware acceleration and local storage. It includes eight AMD CDNA3 MI325X GPUs, delivering a total of 2048 GB of high-bandwidth VRAM. Local storage consists of 28608 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, enabling high-speed data access directly on the physical machine.

Given the high density of compute, memory, and GPU resources, this bare metal configuration offers high resource density for massive parallel processing tasks. It is designed for large-scale workloads such as deep learning training, large language model (LLM) execution, high-performance computing (HPC), and massive database deployments requiring substantial local storage and GPU acceleration.
description: The Vultr vbm-256c-3072gb-8-mi325x-gpu is a high-density bare metal server designed for intensive computational workloads. It features an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 256 vCPUs (128 physical cores) running at 3.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 3072.0 GB of RAM, providing 12.0 GB per core, alongside a massive local storage array of 28608 GB NVMe SSDs. For hardware acceleration, the server bundles eight AMD CDNA3 MI325X GPUs, offering a total of 2048 GB of VRAM. This resource-dense hardware profile is optimized for large-scale parallel processing, making it suitable for deep learning, large language model training, and complex scientific simulations. The platform also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.
